idea: Weekly surge in labor_jobs Google Trends searches for 'gig economy' signals bullish XLY

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'gig economy'

behavior: A surge in interest for gig economy jobs indicates increased consumer spending flexibility and labor market fluidity, benefiting consumer discretionary sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Higher gig economy interest often precedes consumer discretionary spending increases.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends search interest for 'gig economy' rises more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if interest drops below 5% above baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Gig economy interest fluctuates frequently with labor market changes and news cycles.

idea: Daily spike in freight_logistics RSS counts mentioning 'rail congestion' signals bearish XLI

data.source: RSS news feed counts for freight logistics keywords

behavior: Sudden increases in rail congestion news indicate supply chain stress impacting industrial production and transportation sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Rail congestion slows industrial supply chains, pressuring industrial sector earnings.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS counts for 'rail congestion' rise 30% above 30-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or when counts drop below 10% above average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rail congestion news is a recurring theme tied to logistics disruptions.

idea: Weekly drop in consumer_stress Google Trends searches for 'credit card late payment' signals bullish XLP

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'credit card late payment'

behavior: Falling search interest in credit card late payments suggests easing consumer financial stress and potential spending rebound in staples.

market_impact.rationale: Lower consumer payment stress supports staple goods purchases.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search interest drops by more than 10% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if interest rises above baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Consumer stress indicators typically oscillate with economic news and payment cycles.

idea: Weekly surge in political_insider_filing counts for 'energy sector insider buys' signals bullish XLE

data.source: Public political insider filing counts for energy sector buys

behavior: Increased insider buying in energy stocks often precedes positive sector momentum due to confidence in fundamentals or policy tailwinds.

market_impact.rationale: Insider buying signals confidence and possible near-term positive catalysts.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly insider buy filings for energy sector rise 20% above 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 6 weeks or if filings fall below 5% above average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Insider filing activity is steady enough to produce several signals per quarter.

idea: Daily spike in local_economy_weirdness news counts mentioning 'small town broadband outage' signals bearish XLC

data.source: RSS news feed counts for broadband outage in small towns

behavior: Broadband outages in small towns can disrupt communications and productivity, impacting communication services sector sentiment.

market_impact.rationale: Telecom disruptions reduce service reliability and investor confidence.

trade_logic.entry: If daily news counts mentioning small town broadband outage rise by 25% above 30-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 4 trading days or when counts return to baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Broadband outage events are frequent enough in regional news cycles.

idea: Weekly increase in macro_input_pressure FRED series for Producer Price Index signals bearish XLB

data.source: FRED weekly Producer Price Index (PPI) data

behavior: Rising producer prices increase input costs for materials companies, pressuring margins and stock performance.

market_impact.rationale: Higher input inflation squeezes materials sector profitability.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly PPI increases more than 0.5% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if PPI growth slows below 0.1%

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: PPI data is regularly updated and exhibits frequent moderate changes.

idea: Weekly surge in travel_mobility Google Trends searches for 'road trip' signals bullish XLY

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'road trip'

behavior: Rising interest in road trips signals increased consumer discretionary travel spending potential.

market_impact.rationale: More road trips increase discretionary spending on travel-related goods and services.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search interest rises more than 20% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if interest drops below baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and event-driven travel trends cause frequent interest spikes.

idea: Daily spike in consumer_stress Google Trends searches for 'used car price drop' signals bullish XLY

data.source: Google Trends daily search interest for 'used car price drop'

behavior: Sudden search spikes for falling used car prices indicate easing cost pressure on consumers, boosting discretionary budgets.

market_impact.rationale: Lower used car prices relieve financial pressure, increasing discretionary spending capacity.

trade_logic.entry: If daily search interest rises 25% above 14-day moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if interest falls below average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Used car market and consumer cost searches fluctuate with supply and economic news.

idea: Weekly surge in attention_sentiment RSS counts mentioning 'pharmaceutical pricing scandal' signals bearish XLV

data.source: RSS news feed counts for pharmaceutical pricing scandal news

behavior: Spikes in scandal-related news drive negative sentiment on healthcare sector stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Reputational damage and regulatory risk weigh on healthcare stock valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS counts for pharmaceutical pricing scandal increase 40% above 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 6 weeks or if counts fall below 10% above average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare controversies are recurring and frequently covered in news.

idea: Daily spike in earthquake_activity near key freight hubs signals bearish XLI

data.source: USGS earthquake activity daily data near major freight hubs

behavior: Sudden increase in earthquake frequency near freight hubs signals potential logistical disruptions.

market_impact.rationale: Freight interruptions from natural disasters hurt industrial supply chains and profits.

trade_logic.entry: If daily earthquake count near major hubs exceeds 2 standard deviations above 30-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or when activity returns to baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake activity near hubs is variable and can spike multiple times yearly.

idea: Weekly decline in labor_jobs Google Trends searches for 'temporary staffing' signals bearish XLF

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'temporary staffing'

behavior: Falling interest in temporary staffing signals labor market slowdown, pressuring financial stocks sensitive to credit risk.

market_impact.rationale: Weaker labor demand increases default risk and reduces financial sector optimism.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search interest declines more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if interest recovers above baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market interest fluctuates with economic cycles and hiring trends.

idea: Weekly spike in macro_input_pressure FRED series for natural gas prices signals bullish XLE

data.source: FRED weekly natural gas price data

behavior: Rising natural gas prices benefit energy producers and related energy sector stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Higher input prices increase revenues and margins for energy companies.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly natural gas prices increase more than 5% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if prices decline below 1% growth

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Natural gas prices are volatile and frequently trend in short bursts.

idea: Daily spike in travel_mobility RSS counts mentioning 'car rental shortages' signals bullish XLY

data.source: RSS news feed counts for car rental shortage news

behavior: News of car rental shortages indicates strong travel demand and higher discretionary spending.

market_impact.rationale: High travel demand boosts consumer discretionary spending on travel services.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS counts for car rental shortage rise 30% above 14-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if counts fall below average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Car rental shortages occur periodically around peak travel seasons.

idea: Weekly surge in attention_sentiment RSS counts mentioning 'cybersecurity regulation' signals bearish XLC

data.source: RSS news feed counts for cybersecurity regulation news

behavior: Increased news about cybersecurity regulation introduces compliance costs and uncertainty for communication services.

market_impact.rationale: Regulatory pressure can reduce margins and innovation in telecom and media sectors.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS counts for cybersecurity regulation rise 35% above 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 5 weeks or if counts fall below 10% above average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regulatory news about cybersecurity fluctuates with policy cycles.

idea: Weekly surge in local_economy_weirdness RSS counts mentioning 'unexpected city tax hike' signals bearish XLRE

data.source: RSS news feed counts for unexpected city tax hikes

behavior: Unexpected local tax hikes reduce disposable income and commercial real estate demand, pressuring REITs.

market_impact.rationale: Higher local taxes increase operating costs and reduce property values.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS counts for unexpected city tax hikes rise 40% above 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 6 weeks or if counts normalize

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Local tax policy surprises are reported regularly in regional media.
